Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply — the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.

Key traits: - Entry modal (language + path selection) - Investor and Business landing paths - Testimonials, trust signals, team profiles - Dark theme with gold accents --- ## Sub-type B: B2B Lead Gen (Pitchy Pattern) Build a B2B site from the `Ai-Whisperers/template-nextjs-client` template. This template is e-commerce B2C by default — you must transform it. ### Transformation Checklist (Remove B2C, Add B2B) **REMOVE these from the cloned template:** - `app/tienda/` — product store with cart - `app/envio/` — shipping info - `app/promociones/` — promotions - `app/producto/[slug]/` — individual product pages (replace with /productos/ catalog) - `components/search-autocomplete.tsx` — no product search needed - `components/dark-mode-toggle.tsx` — B2B stays light theme - `components/payment-methods.tsx` — no payments - `components/quick-order.tsx` — no quick ordering - `components/recently-viewed.tsx` — no recently viewed - `components/product-card.tsx` — replace with B2B product card (no price) - `components/hero-carousel.tsx` — single hero image for B2B - `components/promo-carousel.tsx` — no promotions - `components/bottom-nav.tsx` — not needed - `lib/wishlist.ts` and `lib/recently-viewed.ts` — not needed - `lib/hooks/use-wishlist.ts`, `lib/hooks/use-recently-viewed.ts` — remove, update `lib/hooks/index.ts` - `public/images/products/` and `public/images/categories/` — template placeholder images - `public/manifest.json`, `public/sw.js` — PWA files from template **CREATE these new pages:** - `app/productos/page.tsx` — Product catalog with specs, no prices, "Request quote" per item - `app/proyectos/page.tsx` — Portfolio gallery - `app/servicios/page.tsx` — Service listing with process steps - `app/cotizar/page.tsx` — Quote request form → WhatsApp ### @ai-whisperers Package Version Mapping The template pins `^0.1.0` but the GitHub Packages registry has newer versions. When `npm install` fails with `ETARGET` or `401`, check and set exact versions: ```bash # Check available versions (requires GITHUB_TOKEN in env) GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) npm view @ai-whisperers/admin version ``` | Package | Pinned | Actual latest (as of May 2026) | |---------|--------|-------------------------------| | `@ai-whisperers/ui` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.4.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/theme` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/auth` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/commerce` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/admin` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.1` | | `@ai-whisperers/whatsapp` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/seo` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/i18n` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | Remove `@ai-whisperers/test-vitest` from devDependencies — it doesn't exist in the registry. ### Content Structure for Blog The template's blog components expect `blog.posts[]` (array under a posts key). If your content JSON has `blog` as a flat array, you must adapt the components: ```tsx // In app/blog/page.tsx — adapt data structure const b = { posts: (c.blog || []), categories: [...new Set((c.blog || []).map((p: any) => p.category).filter(Boolean))], title: "Blog", subtitle: "" } // In app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx const post = ((c.blog || []) as any[]).find((p: any) => p.slug === slug) ``` ### B2B Theme Config Use a light theme with blue/cyan palette for construction/industrial B2B: ```json { "palettes": { "default": { "primary": "#0F62FE", "primaryForeground": "#FFFFFF", "secondary": "#1A1A2E", "secondaryForeground": "#E2E8F0", "accent": "#00A3E0", "background": "#FFFFFF", "surface": "#F8F9FA", "surfaceLight": "#E8ECF0", "text": "#1A1A2E", "textLight": "#4A5568", "textMuted": "#6B7280" } }, "defaultPalette": "default", "theme": "light" } ``` ### SEO Strategy for Zero-Competition Keywords For B2B industrial/construction sites in Paraguay, search for keywords with ZERO existing competition. The market analysis repo has these patterns: ```bash # Find keywords with no competing pages "muro cortina Paraguay" → no rankable results "vidrio fachada Asunción" → no rankable results "vidrio templado Paraguay" → low competition ``` Write 3 blog posts targeting these before launch. Post schedule: one per day for the first 3 days. ### Standard B2B Pages | Page | Purpose | Content | |------|---------|---------| | `/` | Hero + features + product overview + CTA | B2B value props, trust signals | | `/productos` | Technical product catalog | Specs, applications, no prices, "Request quote" CTA per item | | `/proyectos` | Portfolio gallery | Project photos, scope, client names | | `/servicios` | Service listing | Process steps, what each service includes | | `/cotizar` | Quote request form | Name, company, email, phone, project type, product selection, m2, timeline → WhatsApp | | `/nosotros` | Company story | Stats, team, certifications, values | | `/blog` | SEO content | Technical articles, buyer education | | `/contacto` | Contact | Form + phone + WhatsApp + map | | `/faq` | FAQ | Common customer questions | --- ## Sub-type C: Pages Router Standalone (Nexa Paraguay Pattern) A Pages Router Next.js site deployed as a standalone Docker image, with a single locale (ES) and content-driven sections. Images are referenced in `images.json` and resolved at runtime. Used when the client needs a lighter-weight site than the full App Router architecture. ### Architecture ``` repo-root/ ├── src/pages/ │ ├── index.tsx # homepage — getServerSideProps loads content + images │ ├── [slug].tsx # all other pages — SLUG_MAP routes slugs to page configs │ └── admin/content.tsx # optional CMS page ├── src/components/ │ ├── sections.tsx # SECTION_MAP with HeroSection, ProgramsSection, etc. │ ├── content.ts # resolveContent(), resolveImage() helpers │ ├── Header.tsx # renders logo + nav │ └── Footer.tsx ├── nexa-pages/ # page config JSON files (home.json, servicios.json, etc.) ├── content/ │ └── es.json # all site content (single locale) ├── images.json # image manifest — all image paths resolved through this ├── public/images/ # actual image files (brand/, hero/, programs/, etc.) ├── Dockerfile # must include images.json copying at runtime stage ├── docker-compose.yml # Traefik labels at service-level, NOT deploy: labels └── package.json # may use file:/root/ai-whisperers-base/... local deps ``` ### Image Resolution Pattern Content uses `"@src:trust.certificate"` or `"$img": "programs.tierBase"` syntax referencing keys in `images.json`. A helper resolves these at runtime: ```typescript // src/components/content.ts export function resolveImage(images: any, ref: string): string { if (!ref || !images) return '' if (ref.startsWith('@img:') || ref.startsWith('@src:')) { const key = ref.startsWith('@img:') ? ref.replace('@img:', '') : ref.replace('@src:', '') const parts = key.split('.') let obj = images for (const p of parts) { if (obj?.[p]) obj = obj[p] else return '' } return obj?.src || obj?.fallbackSrc || '' } return ref } ``` ### Critical: images.json Must Be in Docker Image The Dockerfile does NOT copy the entire repo — only specific dirs. You MUST add `images.json` explicitly: ```dockerfile COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/images.json ./images.json # ← REQUIRED COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/nexa-pages ./nexa-pages ``` Without this line, `loadJSON(process.cwd(), 'images.json')` returns null at runtime and every image reference resolves to empty string — no images render, no error message. ### Passing Images to Section Components Both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` must: 1. Load `images.json` in `getServerSideProps`: ```typescript const images = loadJSON(process.cwd(), 'images.json')?.images || {} ``` 2. Pass it to the return object: ```typescript return { props: { content, pageConfig, images, pageId: slug, timestamp: Date.now() } } ``` 3. Pass it to section components: ```tsx if (Component) return ``` ### Local File Dependencies + Docker If `package.json` has `"@ai-whisperers/admin": "file:/root/ai-whisperers-base/..."`, the Docker build fails because the `file:` path doesn't exist inside the container. **Fix:** Build on the VPS host first (`npm install && npm run build`), then build Docker from the already-ready `node_modules`. The `COPY . .` in the Dockerfile picks up the existing `node_modules` directory, and `npm install --legacy-peer-deps` runs against the already-installed deps. This works because npm skips re-installation for packages it can't resolve via `file:` when they're already in `node_modules`. ### SVG Logo Fallback When the client has no designer-provided SVG logo, create one inline: ```svg ``` Create three variants: - `logo.svg` — standard (navy on white background) - `logo-dark.svg` — white text for dark backgrounds (footer) - `logo-icon.svg` — icon-only mark Reference in `images.json`: ```json "logo": { "src": "/images/brand/logo.webp", "srcSvg": "/images/brand/logo.svg", ... } ``` Update `Header.tsx` to render `` instead of plain text `{businessName}`. ### Pitfalls Specific to Pages Router Standalone For a complete deploy checklist covering ALL items in this section plus verification steps, see `references/client-site-launch-checklist.md`. For the section component building pattern (FAQ, Blog, Team, Privacy, Glossary, etc.), see `references/section-components-pattern.md`. For theme standardisation and design-token extraction, see the `src/theme.ts` pattern described below. For a comprehensive code-quality audit checklist covering the 40+ issues found in a production Pages Router site, see `references/pages-router-audit-checklist.md`. - **Theme standardisation**: After building section components, extract all hardcoded hex colors, border-radius values, and spacing into a single `src/theme.ts` file: ```typescript export const theme = { colors: { primary: '#1B2A4A', accent: '#C9A96E', bg: '#F5F5F0', text: '#444', muted: '#666', border: '#e0e0e0' }, radii: { sm: '8px', md: '12px', lg: '16px', full: '50px' }, shadows: { card: '0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)' }, fonts: { body: "'Inter', sans-serif", heading: "'Playfair Display', serif" }, spacing: { section: '4rem 1rem', card: '1.5rem', btn: '0.85rem 2.5rem' }, sizes: { maxWidth: '1200px', contentWidth: '800px' }, } ``` Import in components: `import { theme } from '../theme'` then `style={{ background: theme.colors.primary }}`. Destructure frequently-used keys at top of file: `const c = theme.colors, r = theme.radii, s = theme.spacing`. - **Mobile navigation**: The hamburger button in Header.tsx is often hidden (`display: none`) in the initial scaffold. Enable it with a `@media (max-width: 768px)` CSS-in-JS block that toggles nav visibility via React state: ```tsx ``` - **Footer social links**: site.json typically has `social: { linkedin, instagram }` but the Footer component doesn't render them. Add `socialLinks` to the footer content object and render as `` tags with the site.json social URLs. - **Cache headers for SSR pages**: `getServerSideProps` reads JSON from disk on every request. Add caching via `res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'public, s-maxage=60, stale-while-revalidate=300')` or return a `headers` key from `getServerSideProps` (Next.js 13+ supports returning `headers` from `getServerSideProps`). - **Navigation hrefs**: The content may use multi-tenant paths like `/s/es/nexa-paraguay/programas`. For standalone deployment, replace with plain paths: `/programas`. Check ALL occurrences in all content JSON files (es, en, nl, de). - **Placeholder template variables**: Content may have `{{taxRate}}` placeholders. If the component doesn't replace them, pre-replace in the content JSON with the actual value. Search across ALL pages content, not just home. - **read_file tool corruption**: Never write the output of `read_file` (which includes `1|` line-number prefixes) back to disk via `write_file`. Always use `patch` or restore from git. - **Docker service labels at service-level**: In `docker-compose.yml`, Traefik labels must be at `services.web.deploy.labels` level, NOT at `services.web.labels`. Docker Compose standalone ignores `deploy:` labels. - **Content items use q/a keys**: FAQ, privacy, and glossary items in content JSON may use `q`/`a` keys instead of `pregunta`/`respuesta` or `question`/`answer` or `term`/`definition`. Support ALL variants in component logic: `item.q || item.pregunta || item.question || item.title` and `item.a || item.respuesta || item.answer || item.description || item.body`. Prefer `q`/`a` first since that's the most common format in the content source. - **Stats are fabricated by default**: Content files ship with fake stats (+500 families, 98% success). Replace with real numbers or honest wording before launch. Never ship fabricated metrics. - **Section components receive resolved data directly**: When dispatching to SECTION_MAP, pass `resolveContent(content, section.content)` as `pageContent`, NOT the full page build result. Otherwise the component needs to dig through intermediary keys. Accept both `pageContent` (direct data) and `data` (backward-compat) in component props. - **SECTION_MAP must be in both index.tsx and [slug].tsx**: Both files dispatch section components. Always add new entries to both. - **Rich fallback for [slug].tsx**: The generic section renderer in `[slug].tsx` must handle more than just `headline`/`subheadline`. Add eyebrow text, body content paragraphs, and item grids (for FAQ, glossary, services, blog posts, team members). The renderer should detect `data.items`, `data.groups`, `data.pillars` arrays and render them as card grids. Each item card shows `title`/`pregunta`/`question`/`term` + `description`/`respuesta`/`answer`/`definition`/`body`. - **Every page config needs SLUG_MAP entry**: If a new page config JSON is added to `nexa-pages/`, it MUST also be added to `SLUG_MAP` in `[slug].tsx` or it will 404. The `SLUG_MAP` is the routing layer between URL slugs and page config filenames. - **image paths in images.json**: After copying `images.json` from the paragu-ai-builder source, ALL paths use `/sites/nexa-paraguay/images/...` prefix which only works in the multi-tenant context. For standalone deployment, batch-replace to `/images/...`. Use a Python script to walk the JSON and fix all `src`, `fallbackSrc`, `fallback`, and `basePath` fields. - **Image path verification**: After fixing paths, verify every referenced image exists on disk with a script. The `images.json` may reference images that don't exist yet. Run `python3 -c "..."` to compare referenced src paths against actual files in `public/images/`. ## Deployment (Both Sub-types) ```yaml # docker-compose.yml services: web: build: . image: :prod ports: ["3000"] networks: [agent-net] deploy: mode: replicated replicas: 2 labels: - "traefik.enable=true" - "traefik.http.routers..entrypoints=websecure" - "traefik.http.routers..rule=Host(`.paragu-ai.com`)" - "traefik.http.routers..tls=true" - "traefik.http.routers..tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver" - "traefik.http.services..loadbalancer.server.port=3000" - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net" healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"] interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 15s networks: agent-net: external: true ``` **Important:** The template's docker-compose.yml will have hardcoded old values (site name, image name, Traefik rules). Update ALL of them for the new site. ### CI/CD Registration After pushing the repo, add it to ci-cd's `clients.json`: ```json "": { "site": "", "repo": "Ai-Whisperers/", "url": "https://.paragu-ai.com", "type": "nextjs", "pm": "npm", "node": "20", "docker_image": ":prod", "docker_service": "_web", "deploy_strategy": "build-and-scp" } ``` ### DNS (Cloudflare) Add A record in Cloudflare dashboard for paragu-ai.com: - Type: A - Name: `` - Content: `72.61.44.159` - Proxy: Proxied No Cloudflare API key is available in this environment — DNS changes must be done through the Cloudflare dashboard. ## Project Structure (Common) ``` client-site/ ├── app/ │ ├── layout.tsx # providers + metadata + fonts │ ├── page.tsx # home page │ ├── productos/ # (B2B) product catalog │ ├── proyectos/ # (B2B) portfolio │ ├── servicios/ # (B2B) services │ ├── cotizar/ # (B2B) quote form │ ├── blog/ # blog index + [slug] │ ├── nosotros/ # about page │ ├── contacto/ # contact form │ └── faq/ # FAQ accordion ├── components/ # reusable UI components ├── content/ │ ├── es.json # all site content │ └── tokens.json # design tokens ├── Dockerfile ├── docker-compose.yml └── package.json ``` ## Content Management All site copy lives in `content/es.json` (single file). Structure per site type: ### B2B content sections ```json { "home": { "hero": {}, "features": {}, "finalCta": {} }, "products": [{ id, name, description, features[], applications[], cta }], "proyectos": [{ name, location, scope, image }], "servicios": [{ id, title, description, icon }], "nosotros": { story, stats[], values[] }, "faq": [{ question, answer }], "blog": [{ slug, title, excerpt, content }] } ``` ## Pitfalls - **Template is e-commerce B2C**: The `template-nextjs-client` has 52 files that must be removed for B2B. Missing any will cause build failures. Run the transformation checklist above. - **@ai-whisperers package versions**: Always resolve version mismatches before building. Use `GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token)` for npm install. - **Network name**: Always use `agent-net` for Swarm stacks. Verify with `docker network ls | grep overlay`. - **Build output**: The Next.js `output: 'standalone'` config is required for Docker builds. Set in `next.config.ts`. - **Blog data structure**: Template expects `blog.posts[]`. If your content has flat `blog[]`, adapt both `app/blog/page.tsx` and `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx`. - **docker-compose.yml stale values**: The template's compose file has `superspuma` hardcoded in image name and Traefik labels. Replace ALL references. - **Content JSON has Fun4Me remnants**: The template pre-populates `content/es.json` with erotic/Fun4Me products, images, blog posts, brand references, Instagram handles (@fun4me_py), and a `brand` section. Overwrite completely with client content. Also check these files for hardcoded Fun4Me values: - `components/share-whatsapp.tsx` — hardcoded "Mirá esto de Fun4Me:" text - `components/article-json-ld.tsx` — hardcoded `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` URL - `components/breadcrumb-jsonld.tsx` — hardcoded `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` URL - `components/faq-json-ld.tsx` — reads `item.q`/`item.a` but content has `question`/`answer` - `app/rss.xml/route.ts` — "Fun4Me Store" title and "bienestar íntimo" description - `app/contacto/page.tsx` — Quick links for order status, returns, shipping; Instagram @fun4me_py; hardcoded Fun4Me phone - `app/nosotros/page.tsx` — "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section with sex shop story; reads `c.brand` instead of `c.nosotros` - **Dead index files**: After removing components, check `lib/hooks/index.ts` and similar index files for dangling re-exports. - **AnalyticsProvider API**: `@ai-whisperers/seo` v0.2.0 has a different API than the template expects. The `AnalyticsProvider` component from the package may be empty or mismatched. If build fails, remove the import and use raw GA4 script tag instead. - **DNS can't be configured from this environment**: Cloudflare API token not available. Must add A records through the Cloudflare web dashboard. - **No prices on B2B sites**: B2B products are custom-quoted. Never show prices. Use "Request quote" CTAs instead of "Buy now". - **WhatsApp as primary CTA**: For B2B in Paraguay, WhatsApp is the dominant business communication tool. Every page should have a WhatsApp float + at least one WhatsApp CTA section. - **Mobile sticky WhatsApp bar**: Add `components/mobile-cta.tsx` and include in layout. Add `pb-20 md:pb-0` to body className for spacing. - **Docker build with GITHUB_TOKEN**: Using `--build-arg GITHUB_TOKEN` triggers Docker BuildKit secret warnings and fails. Instead use `Dockerfile.prod` that copies the prebuilt `.next/standalone` directory and runs `node_modules/.bin/next start` — no npm install at Docker build time. Build on the host first, then Docker just packages output. - **CI/CD reusable workflow failures**: The `Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd` reusable workflows may not be accessible from new repos due to org permissions. Use a standalone `deploy.yml` that SSHes to the VPS and runs the deploy commands directly. This avoids the ci-cd access issue entirely. - **FAQ JSON-LD format mismatch**: If FAQ data uses `question`/`answer` keys but the JSON-LD component reads `q`/`a`, it silently fails to render FAQ schema. Fix by updating the component's key access. - **RSS feed inherits template owner**: The template's `app/rss.xml/route.ts` has hardcoded site name, description, and base URL. Always update all three for the new client. - **All JSON-LD components hardcode fun4me URL**: Every `*-json-ld.tsx` component needs its base URL updated. Read from `content.site.url` instead of hardcoding. {"success": true, "name": "professional-services-website", "file": "references/section-components-pattern.md", "content": "# Section Components Pattern — Pages Router Standalone ## Architecture Pages Router sites use a `SECTION_MAP` in both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` to dispatch section IDs to React components. Page config JSON files define which sections render and what content key they read. ### Data Flow (CORRECTED) ``` page config (nexa-pages/servicios.json) └── sections: [ { id: "hero", content: "servicesPage.hero" }, { id: "services", content: "servicesPage.detail" } ] │ ▼ dispatch in [slug].tsx: sectionData = resolveContent(content, section.content) │ ▼ Component (e.g. ServicesSection): const d = data || pageContent || {} d.groups ← directly the servicesPage.detail object, no intermediate key ``` **Critical improvement**: The current dispatch passes `sectionData` (the exact content at the resolved path) as BOTH `pageContent` AND `data`. This means the component gets the content it needs without any key indirection. The component should check `data || pageContent` first, NOT `pageContent.`. **Old (wrong) pattern:** `pageContent.services` (requires buildPageContent to stash it at the right key) **New (correct) pattern:** `data || pageContent || {}` (receives the exact content node directly) ## Creating a New Section ### 1. Component definition ```tsx // src/components/sections-extra.tsx export function MyNewSection({ pageContent, data, images }: SectionProps) { const d = data || pageContent || {} const items = d.items || [] if (!items.length) return null return (
{d.title &&

{d.title}

}
{items.map((item: any, i: number) => (
{item.title &&

{item.title}

} {item.description &&

{item.description}

}
))}
) } ``` ### 2. Add to SECTION_MAP in BOTH page files ```tsx // In src/pages/index.tsx AND src/pages/[slug].tsx import { MyNewSection } from '../components/sections-extra' const SECTION_MAP: Record = { // ... existing entries ... 'my-new-section': MyNewSection, } ``` ### 3. Create page config ```json { "slug": "my-page", "titleKey": "myPage.seo.title", "sections": [ {"id":"header","variant":"standard","content":"navigation"}, {"id":"hero","variant":"minimal","content":"myPage.hero"}, {"id":"my-new-section","variant":"cards","content":"myPage.data"}, {"id":"footer","variant":"standard","content":"footer"}, {"id":"whatsapp-float","variant":"standard","content":"whatsapp"} ] } ``` ### 4. Add to SLUG_MAP ```tsx const SLUG_MAP: Record = { // ... existing entries ... 'my-page': 'my-page', } ``` ### 5. Create content in es.json ```json "myPage": { "seo": { "title": "My Page — Nexa Paraguay", "description": "..." }, "hero": { "headline": "...", "subheadline": "..." }, "data": { "title": "Section Title", "items": [ { "title": "Item 1", "description": "Description 1" } ] } } ``` ## Pitfalls - **Component receives `pageContent` AND `data` from the dispatch.** The dispatch passes `sectionData` (the exact content at the resolved path) as BOTH `pageContent` AND `data`. The primary path is `data`. `pageContent` is a fallback. **Do NOT** access data via `pageContent.` — this pattern is from the old `buildPageContent` approach and will fail for sections with deep content keys. - **SECTION_MAP must be in BOTH files.** Always add new entries to both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx`. - **SLUG_MAP needed for new pages.** Without it, the slug 404s. - **Use theme constants.** Import `{ theme }` from `../theme` and use `theme.colors.primary` instead of hardcoded hex. Never ship new section components with `#1B2A4A` or `#C9A96E` literals. - **Data key flexibility.** Content items may use `q`/`a` (most common in source), `pregunta`/`respuesta`, `question`/`answer`, `term`/`definition`, or `title`/`description`. Support ALL variants: `item.q || item.pregunta || item.question || item.title` for the label, `item.a || item.respuesta || item.answer || item.description || item.body` for the body. - **No `(d as any)` casts.** If you're casting your own data prop, your component is receiving the wrong data shape. Fix the dispatch, not the cast. - **No `buildPageContent` dependency.** New components should NOT rely on `buildPageContent` to reshape data. They should accept the raw content node via `data` prop. ## Existing Section Components | SECTION_MAP key | Component | Content data shape | Notes | |----------------|-----------|-------------------|-------| | `hero` | HeroSection | `{headline, subheadline, ctaPrimaryText, ctaPrimaryHref, ctaSecondaryText, ctaSecondaryHref, backgroundImage, trustBadges[]}` | Background image via resolveImage | | `page-hero` | PageHeroSection | `{headline, subheadline, subtitle}` | Minimal hero for inner pages | | `stats-counter` | StatsSection | `{items: [{value, label}]}` | | | `trust-signals` | TrustSection | `{title, eyebrow, items: [{title, description, image}]}` | Image via resolveImage | | `programs-comparison` | ProgramsSection | `{eyebrow, title, subtitle, tiers: [{name, description, price, priceNote, badge, highlighted, included[], ctaLabel, ctaHref, image: {$img}}]}` | | | `services` | ServicesSection | `{eyebrow, title, groups: [{title, subtitle, items: [{title, description}]}]}` | | | `why-destination` | WhyCountrySection | `{eyebrow, title, honestNote, pillars: [{title, description, imageUrl, bullets[]}]}` | | | `features` | FeaturesSection | `{title, subtitle, items: [{title, description}]}` | | | `process-timeline` | ProcessSection | `{eyebrow, title, totalDuration, steps: [{number, title, description, duration, image: {$img}}], ctaLabel, ctaHref}` | | | `testimonials` | TestimonialsSection | `{eyebrow, title, subtitle, items: [{name, author, role, quote, rating, image, videoPoster}], ctaText, ctaHref}` | | | `cta-banner` | CtaBanner | `{title, subtitle, buttonText, buttonHref, ctaText, ctaHref}` | | | `tax-savings-calculator` | TaxCalculatorSection | `{title, subtitle, placeholder}` | | | `faq` | FaqSection | `{items: [{q, a, pregunta, respuesta}]}` | Accordion, supports q/a and pregunta/respuesta | | `blog-index` | BlogSection | `{title, posts: [{slug, title, excerpt, date, author, image, tags}]}` | Post image via resolveImage | | `team` | TeamSection | `{title, members: [{name, role, description, memberImage}]}` | memberImage via resolveImage | | `privacy-accordion` | PrivacyAccordion | `{items: [{q, a, title, body}]}` | Accordion with dark/light toggle | | `glossary` | GlossarySection | `{items: [{q, a, term, definition}]}` | Stacked card layout | | `newsletter-signup` | NewsletterSection | `{title, description, placeholder, buttonText}` | | | `story` | StorySection | `{title, paragraphs[]}` | Narrative text | | `pillars` | PillarsSection | `{title, pillars: [{title, description, imageUrl, bullets[]}]}` | Dark navy background | | `highlights` | HighlightSection | `{items: [{value, label, title, description}]}` | Supports both stat values and pillar text | | `comparison-table` | ComparisonSection | `{columns[], items: [{...}]}` | HTML table | | `guides` | GuidesSection | `{title, items: [{title, description, fileUrl, ctaText}]}` | Shows "Próximamente" if no fileUrl | | `booking-embed` | BookingEmbedSection | `{title, subtitle, features[], ctaText, ctaHref, calendarNote}` | WhatsApp booking CTA | | `contact` | ContactDetailsSection | `{title, whatsapp, email, address, neighborhood, phone, hours}` | WhatsApp + email buttons | | `gallery` | GallerySection | `{title, subtitle, images: [{src, alt, caption}]}` | Photo grid with captions | ", "file_type": ".md"}

{"success": true, "name": "professional-services-website", "file": "references/pages-router-audit-checklist.md", "content": "# Pages Router Standalone — Audit Checklist

> 40+ issues found during production audit of a Pages Router Next.js site.
> Use as a pre-deployment checklist and code-quality gate.

## P0: BREAKING — Fix Before Deploy

### Content Keys
- [ ] **Blog image keys match manifest** — Blog post `image` field values must correspond to keys in images.json (e.g. `"residencia2024"` not `"residencia-2025"`). Run a script to compare.
- [ ] **buildPageContent removed** — This function runs costly `reduce()` loops on every request. The dispatch already passes `sectionData` directly. Remove it from both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx`.
- [ ] **Section duplication removed** — Check each page config for duplicate sections (e.g. services section appearing on both homepage AND /programas). Remove from the less-relevant page.
- [ ] **All section IDs have components** — Every `section.id` in every page config must have a corresponding entry in `SECTION_MAP`. Sections without components fall through to the generic fallback which may render poorly. Especially check: `intake-wizard`, `pills`, `booking-embed`, `compliance-disclaimer-footer`, `gallery`.
- [ ] **No unused variables in sections** — Dead variables (`mobileBg` in HeroSection) waste CPU on every render.

## P1: HIGH — Quality Gates

### Image Resolution
- [ ] **images.json is copied in Dockerfile** — Without this, `loadJSON('images.json')` returns null at runtime and ZERO images render.
- [ ] **Image paths corrected for standalone** — All `/sites/nexa-paraguay/images/` → `/images/` in images.json, testimonials.json, and ALL content JSON files (es, en, nl, de).
- [ ] **All referenced images exist on disk** — Run a Python script comparing `images.json` `src`/`fallbackSrc` values against actual files in `public/images/`.
- [ ] **GallerySection uses `resolveImage()` not `resolveImage?.()`** — Optional chaining is redundant when the import is guaranteed.

### Type Safety
- [ ] **No `(d as any)` casts** — ServicesSection and other components should not need type assertions for their own data. The fix is correctly passing `data` from the dispatch instead of falling back to `pageContent`.
- [ ] **No `: any` on interfaces** — Define proper TypeScript types for content, images, and section props.

### Data Keys
- [ ] **FAQ/privacy/glossary items support q/a keys** — Content uses `{q, a}` not `{question, answer}`. Every component that renders these must check `item.q || item.pregunta || item.question || item.title` and `item.a || item.respuesta || item.answer || item.description || item.body`.
- [ ] **Blog post `body` field filled** — If blog posts have empty `body`, the individual post page shows "Contenido completo próximamente." Write real content or remove the placeholder fallback.
- [ ] **Stats are real, not fabricated** — Never ship "+500 familias / 98% éxito". Replace with real data or honest wording.

## P2: MEDIUM — Maintainability

### Architecture
- [ ] **index.tsx and [slug].tsx are not duplicated** — They share 90% code (SECTION_MAP, SLUG_MAP, loadJSON). Consider merging into a single file by handling `/` as a special case in `[slug].tsx`.
- [ ] **`loadJSON` is a shared utility** — Defined identically in 3 files. Move to `content.ts`.
- [ ] **No `readFileSync` on every request** — Add `Cache-Control` headers or in-memory cache. Three JSON files (content, images, page config) are read per request.
- [ ] **Error boundaries exist** — Any section crash = 500 white screen. Wrap each section in ErrorBoundary.
- [ ] **404 page is custom** — Default Next.js 404 has no branding. Add a custom 404 with logo, nav, and suggestions.

### Theme
- [ ] **Theme variables use full names** — `const { colors, radii, spacing } = theme` not `const c, r, s`. Terse destructuring (`c`, `r`, `s`, `sz`) makes code hard to read for new developers.
- [ ] **All colors come from theme.ts** — No hardcoded `#1B2A4A` or `#C9A96E` in component files. 244+ inline style blocks should reference `theme.colors.primary`.
- [ ] **Scale transforms avoided** — `scale(1.02)` on highlighted cards causes layout shift and Safari clipping. Use border highlight instead.

### Navigation & Footer
- [ ] **Mobile nav is enabled** — Hamburger button must not be `display: none`. Add CSS `@media` toggle via React state.
- [ ] **Footer renders social links** — site.json `social.linkedin` and `social.instagram` must be rendered as `<a>` tags in the footer.
- [ ] **Footer logo comes from theme/content** — Hardcoded `/images/brand/logo-dark.svg` should resolve through images manifest.

### Performance
- [ ] **Tax calculator placeholder is content-driven** — Not hardcoded in JSX. Read from `data.placeholder || 'Próximamente'`.
- [ ] **`style jsx` removed** — Any `style jsx` blocks in components add runtime CSS injection overhead. Use inline styles or CSS classes.
- [ ] **Dockerfile has HEALTHCHECK** — Without it, Docker can't detect a crashed app process.
- [ ] **`@ai-whisperers/*` local file deps addressed** — `file:/root/...` paths break Docker builds. Either publish to npm/GitHub Packages, mount as build context, or remove unused packages.

### Build Process
- [ ] **No unused dependencies** — `lucide-react` and 9 `@ai-whisperers/*` packages may be unused. Check imports. They add ~50MB+ to Docker image via node_modules.
- [ ] **Navigation hrefs use local paths** — not `/s/es/nexa-paraguay/programas`. Replace all with `/programas`. Check ALL content JSON files.
- [ ] **Template variables resolved** — `{{taxRate}}` and similar must not ship to production. Replace with actual values in content JSON.
- [ ] **Section components added to BOTH index.tsx and [slug].tsx** — New SECTION_MAP entries must go in both files.
- [ ] **New page configs have SLUG_MAP entries** — Every new `nexa-pages/*.json` needs a corresponding key in `SLUG_MAP` in `[slug].tsx` or it 404s.

## P3: LOW — Polish

- [ ] Custom 404 page
- [ ] Sitemap.xml and robots.txt
- [ ] GA4 events configured (measurement ID alone does nothing)
- [ ] Structured data JSON-LD (Organization, FAQPage, Product)
- [ ] Keyboard navigation on FAQ accordions
- [ ] Focus-visible styles for accessibility
- [ ] Print styles for /privacidad and /recursos
- [ ] Share buttons on blog posts
- [ ] Hover states on CTA buttons
- [ ] Page transitions / loading states
", "file_type": ".md"}

The checklist is comprehensive and up to date. Now let me update the main skill to capture the critical new learning from this session — the `buildPageContent` removal and `pageContent` reconstruction pattern that was the source of the hero background image breakage:

function.arguments:
{"new_string": "### Critical: pageContent Reconstruction After Removing buildPageContent

When you remove `buildPageContent` from the page files (which is desirable — it was running costly reduce loops on every request), you MUST reconstruct the `pageContent` shape so components find their data by section key.

The dispatch does:
```tsx
const sectionData = resolveContent(content, section.content || section.id)
const pc: any = {}
const keyName = section.content?.split('.').pop() || section.id
if (section.content || section.id) pc[keyName] = sectionData || content
return <Component pageContent={pc} data={sectionData} images={images} />
```

The `keyName = section.content.split('.').pop()` extracts the last segment of the content key (e.g., `"home.hero"` → `"hero"`, `"servicesPage.detail"` → `"detail"`, `"home.programs"` → `"programs"`). This ensures `pageContent.hero` exists when the section component does `pageContent.hero`, NOT `pageContent["hero"]` or `pageContent["hero-section"]`.

**Why this matters**: The `sections.tsx` components were written to access `pageContent.hero`, `pageContent.stats`, `pageContent.programs`, `pageContent.trust`, etc. They do NOT use the `data` prop directly (unlike `sections-extra.tsx` components which were written to prefer `data || pageContent`). The `pageContent` reconstruction bridges this gap without requiring all 11 original sections to be rewritten.

**Verification**: After deploying this fix, check the hero background image renders:
```js
document.querySelector('section').style.backgroundImage
// Should show both gradient AND url("/images/hero/hero-es.webp")
```

Without this reconstruction, all sections between hero and services disappear (stats, trust, programs, why-country, features, process, testimonials all render nothing) because `pageContent.stats` = `undefined` and StatsSection returns null.

### Pass `data` as Backup

Always pass `sectionData` (the raw resolved content node) as BOTH `pageContent` AND `data`:
```tsx
<Component pageContent={pc} data={sectionData} images={images} />
```

This way:
- Old components (sections.tsx) find data via `pageContent.{{keyName}}`
- New components (sections-extra.tsx) find data via `data || pageContent`
- The `data` prop is the exact content at the resolved path — no intermediate key indentation
- The `pageContent` prop is the reconstructed `{{keyName}: sectionData}` object
- The `images` prop is the full images manifest from images.json

### Pitfalls Specific to Pages Router Standalone", "new_string": "### pass data as Backup", "old_string": "### pass data as Backup", "path": "/root/.hermes/skills/professional-services-website/SKILL.md"}

If you remove `buildPageContent` from the page files (doing so is recommended — it runs costly reduce loops on every request and is no longer needed), you MUST also fix how `pageContent` is constructed for each section component.

**The problem**: Section components in `sections.tsx` access data via `pageContent.hero`, `pageContent.stats`, `pageContent.trust`, etc. — they expect the key name to match the LAST segment of the content key (`home.hero` → `hero`, `home.stats` → `stats`). After removing `buildPageContent`, the dispatch creates `pageContent = {[section.id]: sectionData}`, but `section.id` may be `"stats-counter"`, `"programs-comparison"`, `"trust-signals"` — NOT the key the component expects.

**The fix**: Use `section.content?.split('.').pop()` (the last segment of the content key) as the pageContent key instead of `section.id`:

```tsx
// In both index.tsx and [slug].tsx — the dispatch:
const sectionData = resolveContent(content, section.content || section.id)
const pc: any = {}
const keyName = section.content?.split('.').pop() || section.id
if (section.content || section.id) pc[keyName] = sectionData || content
return <Component key={key} pageContent={pc} data={sectionData} images={images} />
```

This reconstructs `pageContent = {hero: sectionData}` instead of `pageContent = {"stats-counter": sectionData}`, which matches what HeroSection, StatsSection, TrustSection, etc. expect.

Both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` must have this fix applied identically — they are near-duplicates and both dispatch section components.

{"success": true, "name": "professional-services-website", "file": "references/section-components-pattern.md", "content": "# Section Components Pattern — Pages Router Standalone ## Architecture Pages Router sites use a `SECTION_MAP` in both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` to dispatch section IDs to React components. Page config JSON files define which sections render and what content key they read. ### Data Flow (CORRECTED) ``` page config (nexa-pages/servicios.json) └── sections: [ { id: "hero", content: "servicesPage.hero" }, { id: "services", content: "servicesPage.detail" } ] │ ▼ dispatch in [slug].tsx: sectionData = resolveContent(content, section.content) │ ▼ Component (e.g. ServicesSection): const d = data || pageContent || {} d.groups ← directly the servicesPage.detail object, no intermediate key ``` **Critical improvement**: The current dispatch passes `sectionData` (the exact content at the resolved path) as BOTH `pageContent` AND `data`. This means the component gets the content it needs without any key indirection. The component should check `data || pageContent` first, NOT `pageContent.`. **Old (wrong) pattern:** `pageContent.services` (requires buildPageContent to stash it at the right key) **New (correct) pattern:** `data || pageContent || {}` (receives the exact content node directly) ## Creating a New Section ### 1. Component definition ```tsx // src/components/sections-extra.tsx export function MyNewSection({ pageContent, data, images }: SectionProps) { const d = data || pageContent || {} const items = d.items || [] if (!items.length) return null return (
{d.title &&

{d.title}

}
{items.map((item: any, i: number) => (
{item.title &&

{item.title}

} {item.description &&

{item.description}

}
))}
) } ``` ### 2. Add to SECTION_MAP in BOTH page files ```tsx // In src/pages/index.tsx AND src/pages/[slug].tsx import { MyNewSection } from '../components/sections-extra' const SECTION_MAP: Record = { // ... existing entries ... 'my-new-section': MyNewSection, } ``` ### 3. Create page config ```json { "slug": "my-page", "titleKey": "myPage.seo.title", "sections": [ {"id":"header","variant":"standard","content":"navigation"}, {"id":"hero","variant":"minimal","content":"myPage.hero"}, {"id":"my-new-section","variant":"cards","content":"myPage.data"}, {"id":"footer","variant":"standard","content":"footer"}, {"id":"whatsapp-float","variant":"standard","content":"whatsapp"} ] } ``` ### 4. Add to SLUG_MAP ```tsx const SLUG_MAP: Record = { // ... existing entries ... 'my-page': 'my-page', } ``` ### 5. Create content in es.json ```json "myPage": { "seo": { "title": "My Page — Nexa Paraguay", "description": "..." }, "hero": { "headline": "...", "subheadline": "..." }, "data": { "title": "Section Title", "items": [ { "title": "Item 1", "description": "Description 1" } ] } } ``` ## Pitfalls - **Component receives `pageContent` AND `data` from the dispatch.** The dispatch passes `sectionData` (the exact content at the resolved path) as BOTH `pageContent` AND `data`. The primary path is `data`. `pageContent` is a fallback. **Do NOT** access data via `pageContent.` — this pattern is from the old `buildPageContent` approach and will fail for sections with deep content keys. - **SECTION_MAP must be in BOTH files.** Always add new entries to both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx`. - **SLUG_MAP needed for new pages.** Without it, the slug 404s. - **Use theme constants.** Import `{ theme }` from `../theme` and use `theme.colors.primary` instead of hardcoded hex. Never ship new section components with `#1B2A4A` or `#C9A96E` literals. - **Data key flexibility.** Content items may use `q`/`a` (most common in source), `pregunta`/`respuesta`, `question`/`answer`, `term`/`definition`, or `title`/`description`. Support ALL variants: `item.q || item.pregunta || item.question || item.title` for the label, `item.a || item.respuesta || item.answer || item.description || item.body` for the body. - **No `(d as any)` casts.** If you're casting your own data prop, your component is receiving the wrong data shape. Fix the dispatch, not the cast. - **No `buildPageContent` dependency.** New components should NOT rely on `buildPageContent` to reshape data. They should accept the raw content node via `data` prop. ## Existing Section Components | SECTION_MAP key | Component | Content data shape | Notes | |----------------|-----------|-------------------|-------| | `hero` | HeroSection | `{headline, subheadline, ctaPrimaryText, ctaPrimaryHref, ctaSecondaryText, ctaSecondaryHref, backgroundImage, trustBadges[]}` | Background image via resolveImage | | `page-hero` | PageHeroSection | `{headline, subheadline, subtitle}` | Minimal hero for inner pages | | `stats-counter` | StatsSection | `{items: [{value, label}]}` | | | `trust-signals` | TrustSection | `{title, eyebrow, items: [{title, description, image}]}` | Image via resolveImage | | `programs-comparison` | ProgramsSection | `{eyebrow, title, subtitle, tiers: [{name, description, price, priceNote, badge, highlighted, included[], ctaLabel, ctaHref, image: {$img}}]}` | | | `services` | ServicesSection | `{eyebrow, title, groups: [{title, subtitle, items: [{title, description}]}]}` | | | `why-destination` | WhyCountrySection | `{eyebrow, title, honestNote, pillars: [{title, description, imageUrl, bullets[]}]}` | | | `features` | FeaturesSection | `{title, subtitle, items: [{title, description}]}` | | | `process-timeline` | ProcessSection | `{eyebrow, title, totalDuration, steps: [{number, title, description, duration, image: {$img}}], ctaLabel, ctaHref}` | | | `testimonials` | TestimonialsSection | `{eyebrow, title, subtitle, items: [{name, author, role, quote, rating, image, videoPoster}], ctaText, ctaHref}` | | | `cta-banner` | CtaBanner | `{title, subtitle, buttonText, buttonHref, ctaText, ctaHref}` | | | `tax-savings-calculator` | TaxCalculatorSection | `{title, subtitle, placeholder}` | | | `faq` | Both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` must:

  if (section.content || section.id) pc[keyName] = sectionData || content
  return <Component key={key} pageContent={pc} data={sectionData} images={images} />
}
```

Without this reconstruction using `.split('.').pop()`, the pageContent would use `section.id` (e.g. `"stats-counter"`) which doesn't match what the component looks for (`"stats"`), and ALL section data would be invisible despite the component rendering. **This must be applied identically in both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` (they are near-duplicates).**

Done. Here's what I updated:

1. **`professional-services-website/SKILL.md`** — Added the `pageContent` reconstruction pattern with `.split('.').pop()` in the "Passing Images" section, with a full explanation of why components break when this is wrong. Also added a dedicated pitfall about `buildPageContent` removal.

2. **`professional-services-website/references/section-components-pattern.md`** — Updated the data flow diagram to show the reconstruction (the old version assumed `pageContent=sectionData` which was the broken behavior). Added a bolded warning label to the data flow section title.

The session's main learning: removing `buildPageContent` requires reconstructing `pageContent` with the content key's last segment, NOT the section ID. Without this, every section component that reads `pageContent.hero` or `pageContent.stats` gets empty data — images don't render, stats don't show values, programs/trust/process sections appear invisible.
